title: malaxis andicola var. elata
intro: Malaxis andicola var. elata, also known as the Andean greenhood orchid, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid native to the Andes mountains. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Malaxis andicola var. elata include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried and infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in tinctures and infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
